event: Roundhay Garden Scene, the world's earliest surviving motion-picture film, is recorded.
Thomas Edison's Kinetoscope is given its first public exhibition.
The Lumière brothers hold their first public screening of short films in Paris.
Georges Méliès establishes the Star Film Company, pioneering special effects.
The first recorded film in Japan is made, marking the beginning of Japanese cinema.
Georges Méliès releases the historical film 'Cleopatra'.
'A Trip to the Moon' (Le Voyage dans la Lune) by Georges Méliès is released.
'The Great Train Robbery', an early milestone in narrative filmmaking, is released.
The world's first feature-length film, 'The Story of the Kelly Gang', is released in Australia.
Émile Cohl creates 'Fantasmagorie', one of the first fully animated films.
The first movie studio in Hollywood, Nestor Studio, is established.
Charlie Chaplin's Little Tramp character makes his debut in 'Kid Auto Races at Venice'.
D. W. Griffith's highly controversial epic 'The Birth of a Nation' is released.
United Artists is founded by Charlie Chaplin, Mary Pickford, Douglas Fairbanks, and D. W. Griffith.
'The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari', a quintessential work of German Expressionist cinema, is released.
'Nosferatu', an unauthorized adaptation of Bram Stoker's Dracula, premieres.
The Hollywood Sign (originally 'Hollywoodland') is erected in Los Angeles.
Sergei Eisenstein's 'Battleship Potemkin' introduces revolutionary Soviet montage techniques.
'The Jazz Singer' becomes the first feature-length motion picture with synchronized dialogue.
Walt Disney releases 'Steamboat Willie', introducing Mickey Mouse to audiences.
The first Academy Awards ceremony is held at the Hollywood Roosevelt Hotel.
The Motion Picture Production Code (Hays Code) is introduced, though not strictly enforced yet.
Fritz Lang's first sound film, 'M', starring Peter Lorre, is released.
'King Kong' premieres, showcasing pioneering stop-motion animation by Willis O'Brien.
'Becky Sharp' becomes the first feature film shot entirely in three-strip Technicolor.
'Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs', the first full-length cel animated feature in history, is released.
'The Wizard of Oz' and 'Gone with the Wind' are released in a landmark year for Hollywood.
Charlie Chaplin's first true sound film, 'The Great Dictator', satirizes Adolf Hitler.
Orson Welles' 'Citizen Kane' is released, revolutionizing cinematography and narrative structure.
'Casablanca', starring Humphrey Bogart and Ingrid Bergman, premieres in New York City.
The Golden Globe Awards are held for the first time.
The first Cannes Film Festival is held after being delayed by World War II.
The US Supreme Court's 'Paramount Decision' forces studios to sell off their theater chains.
'Rashomon' by Akira Kurosawa introduces Japanese cinema to a global audience.
The first Cinerama film, 'This Is Cinerama', premieres, starting a widescreen craze.
'The Robe' is released, becoming the first film released in the widescreen process CinemaScope.
'Godzilla' is released in Japan, creating the kaiju genre.
James Dean stars in 'Rebel Without a Cause' shortly before his tragic death.
Ingmar Bergman releases 'The Seventh Seal', a masterpiece of Swedish cinema.
Alfred Hitchcock's 'Vertigo' is released to mixed reviews before later being recognized as a masterpiece.
The French New Wave explodes internationally with the release of 'The 400 Blows'.
Alfred Hitchcock's 'Psycho' shocks audiences and changes the horror genre forever.
'Dr. No' introduces Sean Connery as James Bond, launching the longest-running film franchise.
'A Hard Day's Night' starring The Beatles redefines the musical film genre.
'The Sound of Music' is released, becoming one of the highest-grossing films of all time.
The spaghetti western 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly' cements Clint Eastwood's stardom.
Stanley Kubrick's '2001: A Space Odyssey' redefines science fiction cinema.
'Easy Rider' heralds the arrival of the New Hollywood era of independent-minded filmmaking.
'A Clockwork Orange' is released, sparking intense controversy over its depiction of violence.
'The Godfather' is released, setting new standards for the gangster genre.
'Enter the Dragon' brings Bruce Lee global superstardom shortly after his death.
'The Texas Chain Saw Massacre' helps define the modern slasher film.
'Jaws' becomes the first modern summer blockbuster.
The introduction of the Steadicam revolutionizes camera movement in 'Bound for Glory'.
'Star Wars' is released, becoming a cultural phenomenon and changing the film industry.
'Alien' is released, blending science fiction and horror to immense success.
'The Empire Strikes Back' expands the Star Wars universe and deepens blockbuster storytelling.
'Blade Runner' defines the cyberpunk aesthetic on film.
'The Terminator' launches the career of director James Cameron.
'Back to the Future' becomes the highest-grossing film of the year.
Studio Ghibli releases 'Castle in the Sky', its first official feature film.
'Akira' is released, bringing Japanese anime to mainstream international audiences.
Tim Burton's 'Batman' redefines the superhero film genre and blockbuster marketing.
'Goodfellas' revitalizes the mobster genre under the direction of Martin Scorsese.
'Terminator 2: Judgment Day' features groundbreaking computer-generated imagery.
'Jurassic Park' astounds audiences with photorealistic CGI dinosaurs.
'Pulp Fiction' wins the Palme d'Or and cements Quentin Tarantino's status as an auteur.
'Toy Story' is released as the world's first fully computer-animated feature film.
'Titanic' becomes the highest-grossing film of all time and wins 11 Academy Awards.
'Saving Private Ryan' revolutionizes the portrayal of combat in war films.
'The Matrix' introduces the 'bullet time' visual effect to popular cinema.
'Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon' becomes an international sensation and martial arts classic.
'The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring' begins the critically acclaimed fantasy trilogy.
The Academy Award for Best Animated Feature is first presented, won by 'Shrek'.
'The Return of the King' sweeps the Oscars, winning all 11 of its nominated categories.
YouTube is launched, eventually transforming how video content is distributed and consumed.
'No Country for Old Men' wins Best Picture at the Oscars, marking a high point for the Coen Brothers.
'The Dark Knight' and 'Iron Man' are released, cementing the dominance of the superhero genre.
'Avatar' pioneers modern 3D filmmaking and surpasses Titanic as the highest-grossing film.
'Inception' brings complex, original science fiction to blockbuster audiences.
'The Avengers' proves the viability of interconnected cinematic universes.
'Frozen' becomes a global cultural phenomenon and the highest-grossing animated film at the time.
'Boyhood' is released, having been filmed over 12 years with the same cast.
'Star Wars: The Force Awakens' successfully revives the legendary franchise.
The critically acclaimed 'Moonlight' premieres, later winning Best Picture.
'Black Panther' becomes a massive cultural milestone and the first superhero film nominated for Best Picture.
'Parasite' premieres at Cannes, later becoming the first non-English language film to win Best Picture.
The COVID-19 pandemic causes unprecedented global cinema closures and film delays.
'Top Gun: Maverick' becomes a massive hit, credited with revitalizing the theatrical experience.
The 'Barbenheimer' phenomenon takes over global pop culture, with 'Barbie' and 'Oppenheimer' opening on the same day.
